note: per-story (not per-chunk) evaluation, batch=1, natural story length capped at MAX_SEQ; stories requiring >MAX_SEQ tokens under either tokenizer dropped from BOTH models to keep the paired comparison apples-to-apples. Complementary to eval_held_out.py's chunk-level aggregate, not a replacement for it -- answers whether the aggregate gap is a robust per-document effect or driven by a small subset, not whether it would replicate under a different training seed.
